specification.topic-statement: The action dispatcher routes each decoded input action to the appropriate REPL behavior, with state-dependent handling for interruption, scrolling, completion, and text editing.

scope.in-scope: The dispatch table covering all recognized action types, state-dependent branching (agent state, viewport offset, active completion), the priority ordering for actions with multiple possible targets, and the handling of unrecognized actions.
scope.boundary: How raw terminal input is decoded into semantic action types is handled by the input parser and is out of scope here.
The mechanics of scroll offset adjustment and auto-scroll behavior are covered by the viewport specification.
The mechanics of completion candidate navigation and item commitment are covered by the tab completion specification.
The mechanics of cursor movement and text insertion within the input buffer are covered by the input cursor specification.
How a submitted user message is assembled into an LLM API request is covered by the LLM request specification.

behavior.dispatch-overview: On each input event, the dispatcher receives a single action value and selects behavior via a switch statement covering all recognized action types. Actions that are not recognized are silently ignored with no state change.
behavior.enter-action:
When the Enter action is received, the current input buffer text is submitted as a user message and an LLM request is initiated, provided the input buffer is non-empty and the agent is in the idle state.
The input buffer is cleared and the viewport is scrolled to the bottom as part of the submission.
behavior.escape-action:
The Escape action is state-dependent:
- If the agent is waiting for an LLM response or executing a tool, the in-flight operation is interrupted. - If a completion menu is active, the completion is dismissed. - Otherwise, Escape has no effect.
behavior.ctrl-c-action: The Ctrl+C action unconditionally sets the quit flag, which causes the application to exit on the next event loop iteration. If the agent is waiting for an LLM response or executing a tool, the in-flight operation is also interrupted before the quit flag is set.
behavior.vertical-arrow-actions:
The up and down arrow actions are dispatched to one of three targets in priority order:
1. **Viewport scroll**: if the viewport is scrolled up (offset is non-zero for up-arrow, or the viewport is not at the bottom for down-arrow), the scroll offset is adjusted by one row. 2. **Completion navigation**: if a completion menu is active and the viewport is at the bottom, the selected completion item is moved up or down. 3. **Cursor history**: if no completion is active and the viewport is at the bottom, the input buffer is replaced with the previous or next entry from command history.
If the user has scrolled the viewport upward, up and down arrows scroll the viewport rather than navigating completion candidates, even if a completion menu is visible.
behavior.horizontal-arrow-actions: The left and right arrow actions always move the cursor in the input buffer. If a completion menu is active, it is dismissed before the cursor moves.
behavior.space-action: If a completion menu is active, the Space action commits the currently selected completion candidate into the input buffer and dismisses the menu. If no completion is active, Space inserts a space character at the cursor position.
behavior.tab-action: The Tab action opens or advances the completion menu. If no menu is active, a new completion session is started for the current input. If a menu is already active, the next candidate is selected.
behavior.ctrl-n-ctrl-p-actions:
The Ctrl+N and Ctrl+P actions are explicit no-ops. They are recognized by the dispatcher but produce no state change.
These key bindings are reserved but intentionally inactive. They do not navigate history or perform any other action.
behavior.page-scroll-actions: Page Up and Page Down scroll the viewport by one full screen height. Home and End scroll the viewport to the top and bottom of the scrollback respectively.
behavior.text-editing-actions:
The remaining recognized actions operate directly on the input buffer:
- **Backspace**: deletes the character before the cursor. - **Delete**: deletes the character at the cursor. - **Ctrl+U**: clears the entire input buffer. - **Ctrl+W**: deletes the word before the cursor. - **Ctrl+A / Home (line)**: moves the cursor to the start of the input. - **Ctrl+E / End (line)**: moves the cursor to the end of the input. - **Character**: inserts the decoded Unicode codepoint at the cursor.
For all text-editing actions, if a completion menu is active it is dismissed before the edit is applied, except for character insertion which may trigger a completion refresh.
behavior.auto-scroll-on-input: Any action that modifies the input buffer or submits a message resets the viewport scroll offset to zero (bottom of scrollback), ensuring the user always sees the most recent content while typing.
